behavior_summary: This script implements Vardion's dialogue and short bark utilities. The primary routine (U8P_VARDION2::func0080) runs the NPC's conversation loop: it builds a menu of player responses (including a dynamically constructed 'I am {name}.' option), captures the player's choice, and branches to set or clear NPC attitude flags (vardionAvaName, vardionMad) and possibly hand off to quest/scene logic.
Helper functions produce randomized barks and alternate dialogue branches (U8P_VARDION2::func1DAD, U8P_VARDION2::func2347) used for flavor, dismissal, and exit lines.

Two named dialogue helpers implement alternate conversational flows: U8P_VARDION2::func25A0 builds a conciliatory/escalation menu that can calm or anger Vardion (including setting vardionMad and calling Npc::setTarget when fully hostile), while U8P_VARDION2::func2B0E is an urgent quest-focused routine forcing the player to provide a Truename and setting several quest/attitude flags (e.g., acolyteKilled, vardionFalse, vardionDolt/Fool/Stupid) and calling DAEMSCEN scene functions for outcomes.

Menus and confirmations (spawn->FREE::askYesNo) are used to confirm obedience and branch between friendly or hostile outcomes; process_result() is consulted after confirmations to decide the follow-up.
notable_control_flow: U8P_VARDION2::func0080 enters a ConversationLoop that repeatedly Ask()s a constructed menu (local3) and uses SuspendAssign to capture the selected menu string in local2, exiting the loop when local2 is set to an empty string or "Never mind.".

Menu construction is dynamic: U8P_VARDION2::func25A0 and U8P_VARDION2::func2B0E union/remove items from menu arrays based on global flags (vardionMad, baneJoined, vardionDolt/Fool/Stupid) before presenting the Ask.
Yes/No confirmation (spawn->FREE::askYesNo) is used in U8P_VARDION2::func0080 and U8P_VARDION2::func2B0E; the result is read with process_result() to choose immediate compliance vs. escalation paths.

Hostile escalation sets vardionMad to higher numeric levels and often calls Npc::setTarget(this, player) to prepare for combat/hostile behavior; some branches call external scene manager routines (DAEMSCEN::func014B, DAEMSCEN::func0B96) for quest consequences.
open_questions: Exact numeric semantics of vardionMad (the script writes multiple numeric values: 0x00, 0x02, 0x03) — what exact behavior ranges (e.g., dialogue gating vs. combat) map to those numeric levels in the engine?

Precise effect of VARDION::func073C: metadata shows it is called after apology in U8P_VARDION2::func25A0 but the handoff purpose (continue dialog, change scene, or start quest) is inferred, not explicit.
DAEMSCEN::func0B96 appears in metadata but its precise role in U8P_VARDION2::func2B0E branching is not fully visible — whether it triggers failure, alternative scene, or some cleanup is unclear.
Some Unknown nodes/suspend remnants exist in U8P_VARDION2::func0080 and helpers; whether they imply additional hidden side effects (timing, animations, or other engine calls) is uncertain.

main_function.name: U8P_VARDION2::func0080
main_function.purpose: Primary dialogue handler for NPC Vardion: constructs and presents response menus (including a dynamically built player-name option), captures and strcmp-branches on player choices, updates persistent NPC flags (vardionAvaName, vardionMad), and invokes confirmations or helper dialogue/scene routines as needed.
entry_conditions: Triggered by player-initiated conversation with Vardion (dialogue entry).
Checks vardionMet to select special first-meeting introductory behavior.
Adapts presented options based on vardionMad and other story flags (so the same function covers normal, first-meet, and angered states).

function_map.name: U8P_VARDION2::func0080
function_map.role: Conversation driver for Vardion (menu building, branching, flag updates, confirmations)

function_map.name: U8P_VARDION2::func1DAD
function_map.role: Randomized short-bark generator (farewells, threats, flavor lines) used for quick exits and flavor

function_map.name: U8P_VARDION2::func2347
function_map.role: Stern/dismissive bark generator used to admonish, scold, or force a polite exit

function_map.name: U8P_VARDION2::func25A0
function_map.role: Secondary dialogue branch that builds an apology/escalation menu, applies vardionMad transitions, and can call VARDION::func073C or set Npc::setTarget for hostility

function_map.name: U8P_VARDION2::func2B0E
function_map.role: Urgent/quest dialogue that pressures the player for a Truename, sets multiple quest/attitude flags, and calls DAEMSCEN scene manager functions for outcomes

function_map.name: getName
function_map.role: Engine/API call to retrieve player's name for inclusion in dialogue strings

function_map.name: spawn->FREE::askYesNo
function_map.role: Presents a Yes/No confirmation dialog and returns result to be read by process_result()

function_map.name: process_result
function_map.role: Reads the boolean result of the most recent confirmation/ask to branch dialog flow

function_map.name: Npc::setTarget
function_map.role: Set NPC's target to the player (used to escalate to hostile behavior)

function_map.name: DAEMSCEN::func014B
function_map.role: Scene/daemon call used to advance quest consequences (called when acolyte is killed in U8P_VARDION2::func2B0E)

function_map.name: DAEMSCEN::func0B96
function_map.role: Another scene/daemon call referenced by urgent dialogue branches (likely handles alternate quest outcomes or cleanup)

function_map.name: urandom
function_map.role: Random integer generator used by bark helpers to pick alternative lines

internal_calls.purpose: Produce a short exit/bark when a conversation branch terminates politely (e.g., "Never mind.")
Hand off to a follow-up conversation path that builds an apology/escalation menu
Call stern/dismissive bark when Ask returns empty or to force exit
Continue or transition dialog/scene after the player apologizes (hand-off inferred)
Use short barks for flavor/branch exits (shared utility)

external_calls.purpose: Retrieve player's name to construct the 'I am {name}.' menu option
Prompt the player with a Yes/No confirmation when Vardion demands obedience; result read with process_result()
Read the Yes/No answer to branch into compliance vs hostility
Target the player when Vardion becomes fully hostile (prepare combat/hostile AI)
Advance scene/quest consequences when the player selects the Sabriane option (acolyteKilled branch)
Referenced by urgent dialogue branches for additional scene logic (precise role uncertain)

reads.name: vardionMet
reads.likely_effect: Selects first-meeting introductory dialog versus normal conversation

reads.name: vardionMad
reads.likely_effect: Read to decide menu construction and tone; higher values correspond to escalated hostility

reads.name: vardionHome
reads.likely_effect: Read by bark helpers to select home-origin suffixes/titles in barks (e.g., Earthling/Britannian/Offworlder)

reads.name: baneJoined
reads.likely_effect: Read to determine whether to include the 'Sabriane.' option in the Truename menu (quest gating)

reads.name: vardionDolt
reads.likely_effect: Read to decide whether the insulting 'Bane.' option is available in the Truename menu

reads.name: vardionFool
reads.likely_effect: Read to decide whether the 'Daemion.' insult option is offered

reads.name: vardionStupid
reads.likely_effect: Read to decide whether the 'Morduin.' insult option is offered

reads.name: vardionFalse
reads.likely_effect: Read to determine whether 'I don't know it yet.' is a first warning or should escalate to anger

writes.name: vardionAvaName
writes.likely_effect: Set when the player claims the Avatar identity (records recognition for later dialog branching)

writes.name: vardionMad
writes.likely_effect: Updated to different numeric levels to escalate or de-escalate Vardion's anger/hostility (0x00 calm, 0x02 escalated, 0x03 hostile inferred)

writes.name: vardionDolt
writes.likely_effect: Set when player answers with the insulting 'Bane.' option (records insult)

writes.name: vardionFool
writes.likely_effect: Set when player answers with the insulting 'Daemion.' option (records insult)

writes.name: vardionStupid
writes.likely_effect: Set when player answers with the insulting 'Morduin.' option (records insult)

writes.name: vardionFalse
writes.likely_effect: Set when player first replies 'I don't know it yet.' to mark a warning was given

writes.name: acolyteKilled
writes.likely_effect: Set when player selects 'Sabriane.' in the Truename menu to record a lethal scene/quest outcome

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: This script implements Vardion's dialogue and short bark utilities. The primary routine (U8P_VARDION2::func0080) runs the NPC's conversation loop: it builds a menu of player responses (including a dynamically constructed 'I am {name}.' option), captures the player's choice, and branches to set or clear NPC attitude flags (vardionAvaName, vardionMad) and possibly hand off to quest/scene logic.
